headline: A US government export-control order forcing Anthropic to pull Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominated the month while diffusion-based text generation crossed 1,000 tokens per second.
body: The Fable 5 and Mythos 5 withdrawal, attributed to an export-control directive and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability, drew sustained coverage across multiple channels framing it as a precedent for government control over frontier model access. On the inference front, Google's DiffusionGemma and Xiaomi's 1-trillion-parameter MiMo V2.5 Pro both reported speeds above 1,000 tokens per second, the former using uniform state diffusion and the latter combining FP4 quantization, speculative decoding, and persistent GPU kernels. Among breakouts, system architecture and KV cache quantization led the technique rotation, while Model Context Protocol entered as a new technique tied to aligning AI agents with design systems. Vector database and web UI broke out on the tooling side.
